page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-192 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz, this instance allocates 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The architecture is supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Single-core performance is more moderate, placing in the average tier for stress-ng single-core metrics. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with both read and write large-block operations scoring in the top 10%, despite the overall Passmark memory score landing in the average category.

The hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dense parallel processing without the overhead of large memory footprints, as it maintains a 2.0 GB RAM per vCPU ratio. Advanced instruction support is extensive, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for deep learning. This is reflected in top-tier LLM inference prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models.

Tradeoffs include the complete absence of local storage, requiring external block storage attachments, and the lack of hardware GPU acceleration. This server is optimized for high-throughput workloads such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-192 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server configured with 192 vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM. It utilizes an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, focusing resources entirely on raw CPU compute density. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong cryptographic execution, though single-core performance remains in the average tier. With built-in Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512 support, it excels at parallel processing tasks. This instance is designed for CPU-intensive workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference, offering a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for highly parallelized tasks that do not require massive memory-to-core ratios.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-192 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 192 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-192 features 192 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for high-throughput parallel computing.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 192 vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 384 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated mathematical and AI workloads
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based inference
